reviews.review_msg: shocked to see the ratings were low for Weiran, had her for two classes and shes quite a good professor. Shes concise, she knows what shes doing, doesnt freeze up when a student asks her a question and lots of bonus/chances to improve grade. I would take her for upper div courses for sure

Weiran is very approachable and passionate about the subject. It's a hard course but if you go to office hours and pay attention in class Its very doable. Quizzes are hard but lots of homework and quiz retakes for partial marks.

Weiran is an impressive prof who lectures without notes, unlike most profs, and explains concepts clearly. The course is about 8/10 in difficulty—tough if you struggled with Math 251. Attend every class since notes aren't posted. Quizzes and tutorials can be tricky, so study ahead. Stay on top of things! Overall, goated prof!

shes the human wolfram alpha with logic and doesnt make mistakes like wolfram sometimes does, does the lecture off the top of her head and doesnt miss anything from the textbook. lots of opportunities to get bonus marks, really clear lectures. If you have weiran for math consider that a W.  math 252 in general though is a big workload.
Amazing professor, exams can be hard, but she's passionate and engaging as a lecturer.

some of the assignments and quizzes can be on the harder side, however you can tell she knows her stuff. She walks in with just chalk and then does the whole divergence theorem, applications, examples, and how it correlates to previous stuff off the top of her head w no errors. Really solid lecturer compared to other math profs

as a lecturer shes really good and any question you ask she has an answer whereas some profs would fumble. She gives lots of opportunity for extra credit and her notes are clear. I would just say the quizzes are on the harder side and the midterms are fair but a tad difficult. DO AS MUCH PRACTICE POSSIBLE

I had weiran for calc 3 and 4 and honestly shes a really solid professor, clear grading outcomes, lectures well and provides alternative ways to explain concepts you can tell she knows her stuff. I would say quizzes are really difficult and she expects more than whats taught in class sometimes but shes good tbh
Pretty sure shes a spy yikes
Fantastic lecturer. Truly an expert

shes ok at teaching, midterms are somewhat reasonable but the weekly quizzes are actually horrendous. The quizzes are always filled with some random really difficult question we havent seen. if you HAD to take this prof sure, if you can take another maybe go with the other prof
Quizzes are hard!!! Run away!!!
So bad!!!!! Horrible!!!
doesn't post notes. literally no reason not to, just gonna make students show up to class sick
Lots of self learning
Doesn't post lecture note and her lectures were very confusing

this is a review for math 320. dr sun is great. shes extremely helpful in office hours, so i highly encourage you to go. she doesn't post notes. shes always willing to stop and take the time to iron out confusion in lectures. exams are heavily based on homework/tutorial questions. lectures can sometimes be hard to follow for harder topics.

Dr. Sun's intelligence is inspiring, her lectures are captivating, and she is caring. Her assignments are tough sometimes, but she's always accessible via email, during office hours, or after class. 418 is a tricky course, so be ready to review MATH251&MATH310. She doesn't post lecture notes, but this was an incentive to pay attention in class.

Professor Sun is easily the worst professor I have ever had in my entire SFU career. She gives insanely hard exams and has a very firm and cold demeanour. Makes course content harder than it has to be and purposely tries to confuse the learner. I am writing this inform you to drop the course you have with her for your own well being.

She is very helpful, gives you very clear answer to your questions. But make sure you read the textbook, because analytics is not an easy thing to learn AT ALL! You'd better roughly review the sections that she's about to teach, and carefully read textbooks after class (I think it's true for most MATH courses, many reviews didn't realize it)

She teaches the material with much clarity. I love how when presenting a new definition or theorem, she really goes through the intuition before getting to the rigorous bits. It makes it feel like I'm learning something cool, instead of being forced to memorize yet another formula. Also, one of the most organized profs I've had in a while.

the worst prof in math department I have met. The quality of course is terrible. She cannot teach the konwlege clearly. And her notes is confused. what is more, her test is extremely difficult. She give a problem in 50-min midterm which she did the problem using more than 50 min in class and have not solved it. She is even not clearly about wt s t

Calc 3 is a hard class and she doesnt make it any easier. Lectures spend wayyyy to much time going over the very basics and not enough time on the complicated problems you will see in exams. She is very kind and helpful when she had office hours but she wasnt available a lot. Overall a decent prof in a hard class.

Definitely did not study as much as i should've. Dont think the textbook is mandatory but you will need a lot of practice. She is nice but her explanations are a bit hard to understand or she goes through examples very fast. The tests were so much harder than the homework but that might be because its calc3. Study a lot of different questions!!
Pretty bad

She makes the course unnecessarily difficult. Quizzes are very difficult. I knew the answers to several quiz questions but couldn't write them down in time. Lectures: rushed Exams: UNPREDICTABLE questions. I'm a grad student, I did ALL questions in the textbook, STUDIED 7-11 HOURS A DAY (no kidding), got an A- in 152, and I'm aiming for a C

The term "icon" has at times become overused in modern society. However, I do not hesitate to use it to describe Weiran Sun. Her ability to connect with students is unparalleled, and she can make even the most boring material appear riveting. She breathes life into Mathematics in a way other profs simply do not.

Weiran is a good prof and a nice person. She listens to students' suggestions and very helpful during office hours. However, she is extremely tough marker. Generally, if you want a prof who is good at explaining concepts and has a talent for teaching then she is a solid choice. If you are all about getting an A, then it might be a problem.

Only a few weeks into the semester, but I already love Weiran. She's not easy (this is a good thing), but she is extremely organized. This is all that is needed from a professor for the student to succeed. I hope I can take more courses with this professor.

She made this class a lot more difficult than it should've been. Her exams feature lots of questions with some of them to do with definitions and geometric meanings which made them impossible to finish on time, especially her final. Her explanations could be better and lectures were sometimes rushed.
Tests are very long , did not get to complete any of the 2 midterm. However she really let you understand the material that you are studying

Honestly, Weiran Sun is the best mathematics professor I have had at SFU. She displays her concepts clearly and is very mathematically sound. She is an inspiration to me. She knows her material inside out and is always willing to help you understand the material
Awful teachern. Not recommended for calc 251
Hard class taught by an OK teacher. Had to get a tutor. Do the Lon Capa, it will bump your grade up so much if you do well with it.
run while you still can

If you are a Beedie student who is only taking this course because you have to, do not take it with Weiran! Her exams are extremely unfair and difficult. The average for the midterm was 40%! She expects way too much from Calc I students

Atrocious professor, do not take this course with her unless you're a calculus whiz or have no other choice. Her unclear notes and the lack of ability to explain most concepts will leave you confused when it comes to her (very difficult)assignments and exams. Very reluctant to answer questions during lectures and office hours. Avoid like the plague

Professor Sun lacks the ability to be an effective teaching member of the SFU mathematics department. She clearly demonstrates a very strong understanding on the mathematical theories and proofs but has issues relating the topics or presenting them appropriately. I found her lectures to be regurgitations of the textbook with no added explanation.

Lacks the ability to relate to how much students understand and where they are at. Exams are extremely unfair and difficult. Expect to be asked random proofs and derivations.  Many times she said something will be/or not on the exam, the opposite was true. Doesnt seem to be interested in your success.

She is not the clearest lecturer on the planet, and her assignments will leave you bewildered. On the other hand, her final exam was a surprisingly reasonable and straightforward affair. She makes courses harder than they really are.
made the course way tooo hard. Speedy lectures, unpredictable midterm questions. recommend not to take her for 252.
The tests are tough. But she is nice and she is always willing to help students.

It was the worst quality course I had in SFU so far: she was coming often unprepared for the lectures, messed up theorems, examples. It looked like she did not care what she was doing in this course. She was even half an hour late for the exam. Not good.

Midterms were long. Some parts of the final were relevant to the textbook, other parts were tricky. Wish the questions were more like homework assignments, which were rather helpful. Compared to other professors, her lectures could be more succinct.

nothing much to say......her lecture is pretty good and she is really nice. Midterm is long and a lil bit difficult but its aceeptable...The final...speechless...most difficult final ive ever had!.......

Calc 3 IS a tough course, but weiran makes it harder than needed. She is a very nice person, but as a prof, nope. Best just read the book. her mts are very long. Her final was not very long but a few of the questions were weird. No cheat sheets made this course an untamed beast. GL! :)
Dr. Sun is a great lecturer. Calc 3 is a difficult course and there isn't much she can do about that.
Midterms are really long and difficult

Calculus is hard.Do not expect it to be easy unless you are math genius.Her midterms are difficult but if you understand the materials well.You will be fine. Her english is pretty good and no heavy accent among other ESL profs. She makes another grading plan for us so the final worth 85%.
Midterms are difficult and long
VERY VERY LONG MIDTERMS
Very difficult to follow.

Dr. Sun not only exhibits an excellent attitude towards students in class and is equally affable in office hours, she also teaches with demonstrated understanding and genuineity that is nearly impossible to find in school nowadays. Great prof!
Pretty good.
